title: Forms & Validation

sections.markdown: Flutter forms group input fields under a Form widget with a GlobalKey<FormState>, enabling collective validation, saving, and reset.
Forgetting to dispose() TextEditingControllers and FocusNodes — a common leak. Create them in initState (or use a state manager) and dispose them in dispose().
Use autovalidateMode wisely (onUserInteraction, not always), give fields keyboardType/textInputAction for good UX, and validate on the server too — client validation is convenience, not security.

flashcards.front: How do you validate a whole form?
What must you dispose in forms?

flashcards.back: No — always validate on the server too; client validation is for UX.

quiz.explain: The FormState (via the key) validates and saves all fields.

options: A UX convenience; still validate server-side
quiz.explain: Never trust the client; revalidate on the server.

challenges.text: Build a login form with required + email validation.
Add focus traversal and submit-on-done across fields.
Cross-field validation (password == confirm) with good error UX.
Build a reactive form bound to a state manager with async (server) validation.

interviewQuestions: How do you manage controllers and focus?
How do you handle async/server validation?

references.label: Flutter — Build a form with validation
